item.title: Wednesday Memes for the Memeless
item.description:
Can say from experience, the 40s can be rough at times
Thanks for joining me again for another Wednesday dump! May your days be righteous and may all of the good things happen to you!
item.dc:creator: Ashnak83

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
dang
i've been attacked aagain
i'll allow it
why is the river orange though
mmm hmm
depends where you are in the house
nonsense no one does that
i'm still not sure what it does
did they mean news? i hope they meant good news. not that i don't like gnus just not really set up for them right now
how much prep time for kevin?
i beg your pardon
as is tradition
i am known for doing this
tough to decide they all sound great
wait what
i didn't take this pic, i want no part of whatever is going on here
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: I wonder what year on the Germany 1920s-1945 timeline it is right here, right now. Right here, right now, right here, right now, ...
item.description: Remember this from earlier? That post is no longer there. Well, let's graciously assume that OP took their post down for whatever reason. imgur mods wouldn't burn history memes, right? RIGHT? Well, we'll find out, so please check back on this pot in a bit.
item.dc:creator: MyLastAccountWasShadowbanned

item.title: Even the UK's most right-wing newspapers are running anti-Trump articles this morning
item.description:
The Daily Mail, a.k.a. The Daily Heil
From the Daily Telegraph
Yeah, I know the Mirror isn’t a right-wing rag
This one is
The Sun avoided the subject on its front page
item.dc:creator: Dartfordian1

item.title: Bun. Apply directly to the forehead.
item.dc:creator: Yakeshinu

item.title: National Day of Remembrance
item.description: Today is the national day of remembrance for the US Government arresting and interning hundreds of thousands of Americans in concentration camps because of an Executive Order On this day in 1942 FDR signed EO9066 that authorized the interenment of over a hundred thousand Americans of Japanese ancestry (as well as many Alaska Natives) in concentration camps in the desert. I fear we are going to see this again, soon.
item.dc:creator: LitchLitch

item.title: one of the most amazing things the human brain can do
item.dc:creator: pilomotor

item.title: Detroit was flooded and it froze over night. Cars are stuck.
item.dc:creator: Whispersaf

item.title: Fighting the Good fight
item.description: This lone warrior is out in my neck of the woods fighting the Good fight! I saw her while I was driving home from work, and brought her some fries to fuel the fire. I don't have a lot of means and can't make it to the state capital to speak out in person, but it is nice to know that I am not alone out here. As I was leaving, some bald Nazi drove past in his beat up little car, flipped me off, and proceeded to get out of his car next to her and film himself harassing her. I have no doubt that she can handle him, but it is rough being out there alone. Had I not had my child with me, I would still be there.
item.dc:creator: derekthecleric

item.title: Situation update
item.description: Update to my last post, where I talk about upcoming job and being shuffled around at shelters and peoples couches. I finally got a room through governmental help. Thanks guys for sorting me out with food and stuff on the kofi I posted in my bio. I didn't have anything, and I felt like giving up on everything. The help given really did and still does make a big difference to me right now. Thank you for keeping me fed. I cannot wait to start my job and get the paycheck in the end of March. I would not have been able to get this far without the help of the kind people on here.
item.dc:creator: ccdog03

item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: SS United States departs for Florida, to be sunk as an artificial reef.
item.description: One for the history books. SS United States departs Philadelphia for Florida, to be remediated before sinking as an artificial reef. On her maiden voyage in 1952, she crossed the Atlantic in 3 days, 10 hours, and 40 minutes. She beat the previous record set by the RMS Queen Mary by over 10 hours. She was the first American ship to win the Blue Riband in 100 years. She was the largest ocean liner ever built entirely in the United States. She was designed to be converted from a luxury liner to a troop transport ship in the event of war. She was retired from active service in 1969. She was docked on the Delaware River in Philadelphia from 1996 to 2025. She was the fastest ocean liner to cross the Atlantic Ocean. She still holds that record today. #SSUnitedStates
item.dc:creator: RaiseRMSTitanic

item.title: "Neither Rain, Nor Snow, Nor Sleet, Nor Hail Shall Keep The Beaver From Beavering Around"
item.description: #winter #snow #animals #funny #nature
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: Josh Johnson on Kendrick's half time show... as always, nail on head over and over
item.description:
Josh Johnson is so good that kids should be made to watch his sets at school in lieu of Civics classes... always insightful and reasonable.
His current set has some great content around Kendrick's performance, people's reactions to it and how we can be a part of making change in the world around us.
If he wasn't so busy absolutely killing it with his standup work, I bet he'd make an amazing full time host of The Daily Show.
Full set available here - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TgKsG6NZSIo
item.dc:creator: sjhsnz
